meaning: adj.破坏的n.故意或有计划的破坏vi.破坏
examples: This tape will self-destruct in 30 seconds.
Using certain APIs, like credit card checkouts and self-destruct switches is not practical.
Programming them to self-destruct after 26 checkouts is tantamount to asking librarians to embrace entropy.
They have harnessed the power of lasers to find, map and non-invasively destruct cancerous tumors.
The flick would be encrypted and designed to self-destruct, Mission: Impossible-style, after, say, 24 hours.
